text: Shu on Head made a video talking about
issues on the left, mainly things such
as tone policing, virtue signaling, woke
scolding, if you will, and largely
citing the recent Emiru situation, which
if you might recall, Emu got a lot of
backlash from Redditor types and Twitter
police types because during a live
stream, a viewer asked her her
opinion/stance on Immigration and
Customs Enforcement, aka ICE. And Emu
responded something along the lines of,
"I don't follow politics. If you need to
ask a streamer that's not even political
their opinion on political topics, you
need to get off the internet." As
mentioned, this got Emu a ton of
backlash from leftists all over Twitter,
including some of the top leftist
creators that were so mad at Emu because
how dare she not want to comment on this
matter. So, like I said, she covers all
of that, makes a video talking about why
that's not a good look for the left. And
now those same type of leftists are, to
no one's surprise, angry at Shu. How
dare she criticize the left. Oh my gosh.
Here's an example where a
moderatelysized leftist Twitter account
is complaining about Shu's video,
whining and saying, "She's still totally
a leftist, you guys." And their
complaint tweet getting 11,000 likes. It
gets even better when you take a look at
the responses because we've got this
Ezra user saying, "If you actually ask
her what she believes ideologically, she
is pretty leftwing. but because you guys
are such insufferable scolds, no one
wants to be associated with you freaks.
To which the OP responds and says, "I've
learned that anyone who calls people
scolds always turns out to be
right-wing." People would point out how
that's a terrible point by the OP, for
example, because Hassan uses that sort
of terminology, and obviously Assan is
not right-wing. But the OP continues to
get offended saying that they dislike
Hassan because he uses terminology like
that. But again, the point was simply
that someone using that sort of
terminology doesn't intrinsically make
them rightwing. The OP's response here
obviously doing nothing to address that
bad point that they got called out for.
This would result in the OP getting
responses like this. Now, you got
destroyed and ratioed by somebody with
399 followers on your own turf. That
says a lot about the credibility behind
anything you say or post. Recommended
advice, give up. Perhaps less insecurity
to question what you think before you
blurt it out would serve you better.
Ultimately, the takeaway to this is that
these people that are angry at Shuan
Head essentially proved her point. And
the fact that they don't see that is a
massive self-report on how they have
zero self-awareness. That's the latest.
Let me know what you think about the
shoe on head situation. Thank you for
tuning in and I'll see you in the next
